pi_items.source: Verified by reading setup_task.sh which explicitly generates the Proxy CA certificate using openssl at this path.
Verified by reading export_result.sh which uses this path to check for the exported backup certificate's existence and validity.

Verified from the task description which explicitly instructs setting 'security.tls.version.min' to 3. Web searches confirm 3 corresponds to TLS 1.2 in Firefox (contradicting the prompt's 'TLS 1.3' claim, but 3 is the objectively expected input value).
Verified from the task description which explicitly instructs setting 'security.cert_pinning.enforcement_level' to 2. Web searches confirm this value enables strict certificate pinning.

privileged_info_summary: The task data is synthetically generated via setup_task.sh, which uses openssl to dynamically create a proxy CA certificate at /home/ga/Documents/ProxyRootCA.crt. The evaluation script (export_result.sh) correctly expects the ISRG Root X1 backup certificate to be exported to /home/ga/Documents/ISRG_Root_Backup.crt. It extracts the security.tls.version.min and security.cert_pinning.enforcement_level preferences from the Tor Browser's prefs.js file to check against the target values 3 and 2, respectively. A notable contradiction in the task description is that while it correctly instructs the user to set security.tls.version.min to 3, this value actually corresponds to TLS 1.2 in Firefox/Tor Browser, not TLS 1.3.
